what_it_does: Defines an Oxlint adherence configuration for a design system, including React/import plugins, warning rules for restricted imports and raw styling literals, an index-file override, and x-omelette metadata for tokens, token kinds, and allowed font families.
observations: Uses the react and import plugins.
Configures react/forbid-elements as a warning with an empty forbid list.
Warns on restricted imports from multiple app, asset, tweaks-panel, and ui_kits path patterns with a message directing consumers to import design-system components from index.js.
Warns on raw hex color literals, raw px literals, and font-family declarations outside Sora, DM Sans, and JetBrains Mono.
Includes an x-omelette section with an empty components object, a long CSS custom property token list, tokenKinds mappings, and fontFamilies metadata.
Token kinds classify entries as color, spacing, shadow, font, or other.
standouts: Combines lint rules with design-system inventory metadata in the same JSON file.
The import restriction has an escape hatch for index.js, matching the file's own public-entrypoint import message.
The token inventory includes semantic color, spacing, radius, typography, motion, layout, zone, pigment, vi, and spatial naming groups.
conventions: Uses warning severity rather than errors for adherence rules.
Uses CSS custom property names as token identifiers.
Keeps font-family allowlisting aligned with the x-omelette fontFamilies list.
Uses selector-based restricted syntax rules for style-literal checks.
Groups design-system metadata under a nonstandard x-omelette key.
Leaves components metadata empty while tokens and tokenKinds are populated.
concerns: react/forbid-elements is enabled with no forbidden elements, so that rule currently has no visible effect.
The restricted import group contains broad paths and more specific paths under the same broad prefixes, which may be redundant within this file.
The raw px literal selector targets string literals and may not cover numeric style values outside quoted strings.
Token names are listed separately from tokenKinds mappings, creating a consistency burden inside the same file.
notes: This file is a design-system adherence ledger: it encodes lint warnings for usage boundaries and raw styles, plus metadata describing the token surface.
questions: What tool consumes the x-omelette metadata?
Are components intentionally empty or waiting for generated entries?
Should react/forbid-elements remain configured if no elements are forbidden?
Are all tokens in tokens expected to appear in tokenKinds, and is that checked elsewhere?
purpose_vs_actual: The filename and content align: it acts as an adherence configuration with lint rules and design-system metadata.
intention_signals: It appears intended to keep consumers on public design-system imports and token-based styling while exposing a machine-readable inventory of available tokens and fonts.
novelty: The file pairs Oxlint rules with a custom x-omelette design-system metadata block rather than keeping lint policy and token inventory separate.
contribution: Provides a local adherence contract for a fused system to keep: import-boundary warnings, raw style literal warnings, approved font families, and a typed catalog of design tokens.
summary_for_embedding: Oxlint JSON config defining design-system adherence warnings for restricted imports, raw hex and px literals, font-family allowlisting, index.js import overrides, and x-omelette token metadata.
intention_for_embedding: Created to guide design-system consumers toward public index imports and tokenized styling while documenting the available token and font surface for tooling.
